skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Browser automation for AI agents via inference.sh. Navigate web pages, interact with elements using @e refs, take screenshots, record video. Capabilities: web scraping, form filling, clicking, typing, drag-drop, file upload, JavaScript execution. Use for: web automation, data extraction, testing, agent browsing, research.
skill.summary: Browser automation tool for AI agents using Playwright via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All 606 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation (markdown files) and template shell scripts. The external_commands findings are code examples in markdown showing users how to use the infsh CLI tool. The network findings are example URLs in documentation. The filesystem findings are example file paths. The skill itself does not execute any commands - it provides documentation for a third-party CLI tool (inference.sh). Users execute the commands themselves, not the skill.

content.user_title: Automate Web Browsing with AI Agents
content.value_statement: Automate web interactions like form filling, data extraction, and testing without manual browser control. This skill provides AI agents with browser automation capabilities through a simple element reference system, eliminating the need for complex selectors or XPath queries.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to any URL and retrieve interactive page elements with unique @e references
Perform user interactions including clicks, form filling, drag-and-drop, and file uploads
Execute custom JavaScript code on web pages to extract or manipulate data
Take screenshots and record videos of browser sessions for debugging
Maintain authenticated sessions across multiple page interactions
Configure browser options including viewport size, user agent, and proxy settings
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI tool (infsh) to be installed separately
Element references (@e) are invalidated after page navigation and require re-snapshotting
Cannot interact with elements that are not visible or accessible in the current viewport
Dependent on the availability and uptime of the inference.sh service
Session state is lost when the browser session is closed

use_cases.title: Automated Form Testing
use_cases.description: QA engineers can automate form submission workflows across multiple browsers, testing validation logic and error handling without manual clicking

use_cases.title: Data Extraction from Web Apps
use_cases.description: Researchers can extract structured data from web applications that require login, navigating through authenticated sessions to scrape reports or dashboards

use_cases.title: Regression Testing
use_cases.description: Developers can create reproducible browser automation scripts to verify critical user flows like checkout processes or account registration after code changes

prompt_templates.title: Open Web Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Open https://example.com and show me all interactive elements on the page
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to see what elements are available on a webpage before interacting with them

prompt_templates.title: Fill and Submit Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Fill in the login form with username 'user@example.com' and password 'secret123', then click the submit button
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to automate a login workflow using credentials

prompt_templates.title: Extract Data with JavaScript
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to https://example.com/data and execute JavaScript to extract all product names and prices into a structured format
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to scrape structured data from a webpage using custom JavaScript

prompt_templates.title: Record Debugging Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a browser session with video recording enabled, navigate through the checkout flow, and save the video for debugging
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to document or debug a multi-step browser interaction

output_examples.input: Open https://example.com and show me interactive elements
Click the search button and wait for results
output_examples.output:
Action completed: clicked @e3
Waiting for page load...
Snapshot updated: 5 new elements found

Current URL: https://example.com/search?q=test

output_examples.input: Extract all headlines from the page
output_examples.output:
Executing JavaScript: document.querySelectorAll('h2').map(h => h.textContent)

Result: ['Introduction', 'Features', 'Pricing', 'Contact']
Screenshot saved

best_practices: Always re-snapshot after page navigation to get fresh element references, as @e refs become invalid when the DOM changes
Use environment variables for sensitive credentials instead of hardcoding them in automation scripts
Close browser sessions when complete to free up resources and retrieve recorded videos
Start with video recording enabled during development, then disable it for production runs to improve performance
anti_patterns: Do not reuse element references after clicking links or submitting forms - always call snapshot to get new refs
Avoid long session durations without activity - sessions may timeout and lose state
Never commit credentials or API keys in automation scripts that use this skill
Do not attempt to interact with elements before they are visible in the viewport

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the inference.sh CLI tool (infsh) installed. Run 'npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools' for installation instructions.

faq.question: What happens to my element references after I click a link?
faq.answer: Element references (@e) are invalidated after any navigation or DOM change. Always call the snapshot function to get fresh references after navigation.

faq.question: Can I use this to scrape data from websites?
faq.answer: Yes, you can navigate to pages, execute JavaScript to extract data, and capture screenshots. The skill supports authenticated sessions for scraping protected content.

faq.question: How do I handle login forms with two-factor authentication?
faq.answer: The skill supports manual intervention for 2FA - you can pause automation to enter codes, or use TOTP tools to generate codes programmatically. See authentication.md for examples.

faq.question: Is my browsing activity private?
faq.answer: The skill uses the inference.sh service to execute browser commands. Review their privacy policy for details on data handling. Use proxy support for additional privacy.

faq.question: Can I run multiple browser sessions in parallel?
faq.answer: Yes, each session has a unique session_id. You can create multiple sessions and run them concurrently for parallel testing or data extraction.
